courseTitle: 入門電梯簡報 Elevator Pitch

vocabulary.definition: A brief and engaging summary of an idea, project, product, or yourself.
vocabulary.example: An elevator pitch is a brief and engaging summary of an idea, project, product, or yourself, meant to be delivered in 30-60 seconds to spark interest.

vocabulary.definition: Restricted in area or volume; cramped.
vocabulary.example: An Elevator Pitch is a sales pitch conducted inside an elevator to take advantage of the confined space.

vocabulary.definition: To combine one thing with another so that they become a whole.
vocabulary.example: TimeSaver integrates seamlessly with existing calendars and provides smart reminders to keep users on track.

vocabulary.definition: Smoothly and continuously, with no apparent gaps or spaces between one part and the next.
vocabulary.example: TimeSaver integrates seamlessly with existing calendars and provides smart reminders to keep users on track.

vocabulary.definition: The amount of carbon dioxide and other carbon compounds emitted due to the consumption of fossil fuels by a particular person, group, etc.
vocabulary.example: EcoTrack App helps reduce carbon footprint with eco-friendly suggestions.

vocabulary.definition: To promote or publicize something extravagantly.
vocabulary.example: Don't hype up the product or service too much by saying 'this amazing product will save your life'.

vocabulary.zh: 最高級的詞；誇張的言辭
vocabulary.definition: An exaggerated or hyperbolical expression of praise.
vocabulary.example: When presenting your pitch, do not use too many superlatives like 'best in the world'.

vocabulary.definition: Special words or expressions that are used by a particular profession or group and are difficult for others to understand.
vocabulary.example: Avoid using too many business jargon like 'value-added results' or 'cost-effective solutions'.

vocabulary.definition: A suggested scheme or plan of action, especially in a business context.
vocabulary.example: They outline potential expenses and revenue projections, presenting a proposition for collaboration or investment.

vocabulary.definition: A useful or valuable thing, person, or quality.
vocabulary.example: In a business pitch, entrepreneurs highlight their assets, like unique ideas or skills, to attract investors.

vocabulary.definition: A risky or daring journey or undertaking, especially a business enterprise.
vocabulary.example: By showcasing how their assets can generate profit and minimize expenses, they aim to persuade others to join their venture.

sentences.sentence: An elevator pitch is a brief and engaging summary of an idea, project, product, or yourself, meant to be delivered in 30-60 seconds to spark interest.
sentences.zh: 電梯簡報是關於想法、專案、產品或你自己的簡短而引人入勝的總結，旨在於 30-60 秒內發表以激發興趣。

sentences.sentence: TimeSaver integrates seamlessly with existing calendars and provides smart reminders to keep users on track.
sentences.zh: TimeSaver 與現有日曆無縫整合，並提供智能提醒以幫助用戶掌握進度。

sentences.sentence: Remember to smile and maintain eye contact during your pitch!
sentences.zh: 記得在推銷簡報時保持微笑並保持眼神交流！

sentences.sentence: Adjust your pitch to fit your audience and context.
sentences.zh: 根據您的受眾和情境調整您的推銷簡報。

sentences.sentence: In a business pitch, entrepreneurs highlight their assets, like unique ideas or skills, to attract investors.
sentences.zh: 在商業推銷中，企業家會強調他們的資產（優勢），例如獨特的想法或技能，以吸引投資者。
